---
title: "RL and Rollout Planning"
phase: thesis
audience: public
status: current
owner: jan
format: html
---

## RL and Rollout Planning for ARIA-NBV {#rl-rollout-planning-literature}

**Primary sources.** Trajectory Transformer [@TrajectoryTransformer-janner2021], Gumbel-Top-k / stochastic beam search [@GumbelTopK-kool2019], Double DQN [@DoubleDQN-vanHasselt2015], Implicit Q-Learning [@IQL-kostrikov2021], Deep Energy-Based Policies / soft Q-learning [@DeepEnergyPolicies-haarnoja2017], PPO [@PPO-schulman2017], and SAC [@SAC-haarnoja2018].

**Related ARIA-NBV pages.** [VIN-NBV](vin_nbv.qmd), [GenNBV](gen_nbv.qmd), [Hestia](hestia.qmd), [roadmap](../thesis/roadmap.qmd), and [research questions](../thesis/questions.qmd).

### Core contribution

The RL literature is useful for ARIA-NBV only after the finite-candidate rollout substrate is trusted. The current thesis core is not unrestricted continuous control. It is a target-conditioned finite-candidate value problem over offline {{< gls oracle-rri >}} rollout data.

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
  A["Deterministic RolloutTrace"] --> B["Random-valid, one-step greedy, bounded oracle lookahead"]
  B --> C["Temperature-softmax and Gumbel-Top-k rollout data"]
  C --> D["Target-conditioned fitted Double-Q / Q_H over finite candidates"]
  D --> E["IQL as second offline-RL ablation"]
  E --> F["Online Gymnasium / SB3 / PPO / SAC only after a simulator exists"]
```

The mandatory learned value target is a bounded cumulative target-RRI return:

$$
Q_H(s_t, q_t, z_e)
\approx
\mathbb{E}\!\left[
  \sum_{k=0}^{H-1-t}
  \gamma^k r^{\mathrm{target}}_{t+k}
  \,\middle|\,
  s_t, q_t, z_e
\right],
$$

where $s_t$ is the rollout state, $q_t$ is a valid candidate view, $z_e$ is the actor-visible target input, and $r^{\mathrm{target}}$ is derived from {{< gls target-specific-rri >}}.

### Verified paper signals

| paper family | source-backed signal | ARIA-NBV adoption |
|---|---|---|
| Trajectory Transformer | Offline control can be modeled as sequences of states, actions, and rewards; beam search can decode high-return trajectories. | Use bounded rollout/beam search as a planning primitive before training a large sequence model. |
| Gumbel-Top-k | Ordered size-$k$ samples can be drawn without replacement, and stochastic beam search avoids enumerating the full sequence space. | Use Gumbel-Top-k after deterministic lookahead to diversify rollout data and avoid duplicate root-greedy traces. |
| Double DQN | Separating action selection from action evaluation reduces max-operator overestimation. | Use fitted Double-Q as the first mandatory learned {{< gls finite-horizon-q-function >}} method over finite candidate sets. |
| IQL | Offline RL should avoid querying unseen actions; value fitting uses an upper expectile over dataset actions before advantage-weighted policy extraction. | Keep as a second offline-RL ablation after the fitted Double-Q dataset and masks are stable. |
| Soft Q / energy policies | Maximum-entropy policies can represent multimodal action distributions through energy/Q-shaped sampling. | Use as conceptual support for temperature-softmax candidate selection, not as the first thesis algorithm. |
| PPO / SAC | Practical online actor-critic methods assume an interactive reward loop and a simulator or environment abstraction. | Simulator-gated bridge only; not a required quantitative continuous-control result. |

For the Double-Q gate, the finite-candidate target should mirror the Double DQN selector/evaluator separation:

$$
Y_t^{\mathrm{DoubleQ}}
=
r_t
+
\gamma\,
Q_{\bar{\theta}}\!\left(
  s_{t+1},
  \arg\max_{q'\in\mathcal{Q}_{t+1}^{\mathrm{valid}}}
  Q_{\theta}(s_{t+1}, q', z_e),
  z_e
\right).
$$

Invalid candidates must be excluded from the maximization through {{< gls validity-mask >}} values and explicit reason codes.

### ARIA-NBV adoption

- **Thesis-core method:** deterministic oracle rollout, stochastic rollout data generation, and fitted Double-Q / {{< gls finite-horizon-q-function >}} over finite candidate sets.
- **Gated follow-up:** IQL once the offline rollout store has enough behavioral diversity and scene-level split integrity.
- **Stretch/bridge:** PPO, SAC, SB3, continuous action policies, and simulator-backed online RL after a true environment loop exists.

The required ordering is:

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A["Trusted oracle labels"] --> B["Replayable rollout traces"]
  B --> C["Deterministic lookahead upper bound"]
  C --> D["Stochastic rollout coverage"]
  D --> E["Masked fitted Double-Q Q_H"]
  E -. "optional after Q_H" .-> F["IQL / actor-critic bridge"]
```

### Do not adopt

- Do not start with PPO/SAC on an expensive oracle without a simulator-grade reward loop.
- Do not train value heads before deterministic rollouts expose real headroom over one-step greedy.
- Do not let Q-learning evaluate invalid, out-of-support, or ungenerated actions.
- Do not present stochastic beams as a substitute for deterministic oracle lookahead; they are data-diversification tools after the trusted comparison exists.

### Open risks / caveats

- Offline rollout data can be narrow if generated mostly by root-greedy policies; late-branch schedules and random-valid traces are needed for support.
- Double-Q reduces overestimation but does not solve support mismatch by itself.
- Learned values should be evaluated under equal acquisition budget against one-step greedy, oracle lookahead, and random-valid baselines on scene-level splits.
